__NOTOC__ {{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |Mieses Opening| |rd|nd|bd|qd|kd|bd|nd|rd|= |pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|= | | | | | | | | |= | | | | | | | | |= | | | | | | | | |= | | | |pl| | | | |= |pl|pl|pl| |pl|pl|pl|pl|= |rl|nl|bl|ql|kl|bl|nl|rl|= |moves=1. d3 |eco=[[Chess/ECOA|A00]] |parent=[[../|Starting position]] }} == 1. d3 · Mieses Opening == This move is playable but not played much as it does little to claim the center and potentially locks in the light squared bishop. It would normally transpose into the [[Chess/King's Indian Attack|King's Indian Attack]], or with colours reversed, a [[Chess/Modern Defense|Modern]], [[Chess/Pirc Defence|Pirc]], or [[Chess/King's Indian Defense|King's Indian defence]]. Not a very common opening as many player desire to claim the center with a more conventional approach with 1. d4 or 1. e4 == Theory table ==
